_note: Search-friendly soccer dictionary cards for terms commentators and fans use without always stopping to define them.

terms.title: Advantage
terms.hook: The referee can skip the whistle if the fouled team is better off playing on.
terms.summary: Advantage means the referee recognizes a foul but allows play to continue because the attacking team still has the better opportunity.
terms.whyItMatters: New viewers often think the referee missed the call, when the referee may actually be rewarding the team that survived it.
terms.whatToWatch: If the ball pops to a teammate in space right after contact, the referee may spread both arms and let the move keep breathing.

terms.title: Counter-press
terms.hook: You lose the ball and immediately try to win it back before the opponent can breathe.
terms.summary: A counter-press happens right after possession is lost. Instead of retreating, the team that just gave it away attacks the ball and nearby passing lanes at once.
terms.whyItMatters: This is one of the fastest ways to stop a dangerous counterattack before it even gets started.
terms.whatToWatch: See whether the nearest three or four players jump toward the ball together instead of jogging back and resetting.

terms.title: Low block
terms.hook: Sometimes defending deep is the plan, not the panic.
terms.summary: A low block is a compact defensive setup close to a team's own goal, inviting the opponent to have the ball in less dangerous areas.
terms.whyItMatters: If you do not know the term, an underdog can look timid when it is actually executing its strategy almost perfectly.
terms.whatToWatch: Notice how little space exists between the defense and midfield, and how often the favorite is forced to circulate the ball in front of the block.

terms.title: Switch of play
terms.hook: Crowd one side, then punish the space you created on the other.
terms.summary: A switch of play is a pass or sequence that moves the ball from one flank to the opposite flank to stretch the defense and find a freer player.
terms.whyItMatters: It is one of the cleanest ways to turn harmless-looking possession into sudden danger.
terms.whatToWatch: If one side of the field feels crowded, glance to the weak side. The next important player might be standing alone over there.

terms.title: Through ball
terms.hook: The pass is trying to beat the defense, not just reach the teammate.
terms.summary: A through ball is played into space behind or between defenders so the runner can meet it in stride.
terms.whyItMatters: This is one of the most dramatic passes in the sport because the right timing can remove half a defense in one touch.
terms.whatToWatch: Watch the passer's head come up and the striker begin the run almost at the same instant. The timing is the whole trick.
